raw_consumer_statement:
I am writing in response to your letter dated XX/XX/XXXX, regarding the above-referenced accounts. 
While I acknowledge that I originally opened these accounts, I continue to dispute numerous unauthorized transactions that directly caused the charge-off balances you now report and that your collection agencies are pursuing. 

Your letter references that : Barclays previously accepted multiple fraud claims on these accounts ( XX/XX/XXXX, XX/XX/XXXX, XX/XX/XXXX, XX/XX/XXXX, etc. ), yet You still determined that the account and balance are valid, based solely on my personal identifying information matching the application and the absence of a fraud alert. 
That reasoning is not evidence of authorization. Under the Fair Credit Billing Act ( 15 U.S.C. 1666 ) and Fair Credit Reporting Act ( 15 U.S.C. 1681s-2 ), you are required to maintain and provide documentation that substantiates the accuracy of any information you continue to report or collect on. 

Accordingly, I hereby demand that Barclays produce the following documentary evidence substantiating your claim that the disputed transactions were authorized by me : Copies of transaction records for all purchases and payments from XX/XX/XXXX through charge-off on both accounts. 

Merchant documentation, including signed charge slips, invoices, or online purchase authorizations showing my name, device ID, or IP address. 

XXXX XXXX used in your fraud review, including : IP addresses, device fingerprints, and geolocation data of each disputed transaction. 

The originating and receiving account information for all payments you allege came from my external accounts. 

Internal investigation records, including notes, reports, or summaries from your fraud department showing how you reached your determination that the disputed transactions were valid. 

Copies of communications or emails allegedly sent to or received from me concerning these disputed transactions. 

Until Barclays provides verifiable evidence directly linking me to the disputed transactions, I do not acknowledge any responsibility for the charged-off balances.

Furthermore, under FDCPA 1692g ( b ), all collection activity by XXXX XXXX XXXXXX/XX/XXXXXXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX. must cease until Barclays has provided this verification. 
Any continued collection efforts or negative credit reporting without proof of authorization will be treated as a willful violation of the FCRA and FDCPA and reported to the Consumer Financial Protection Bureau and the XX/XX/XXXX XXXX XXXXXX/XX/XXXXXXXX. 


Thank you for your prompt attention.
